European Solidarity Corps The power of together menu Log in General information Young people Organisations Projects General information Young people Organisations Projects The Horse Speaks All Languages JM CAPRIFOLEN Kommanditbolag JM Caprifolen KB Våxtorp, Sweden Apply Description The Horse Speaks All Languages aims to create a safe space and learning environment for people with or without disabilities in mixed groups where we work using the horse as the main tool for integration and creating of projects and methods. Volunteers will empower persons with disabilities to be a part of our community and participate at equal terms, and reduce the stigma associated around persons with disabilities. Your role as a volunteer involves: Offer assistance in our social groups in the areas of circus, horse vaulting, drama and dance. Be a part of “farm life” where you work around horses in coherence with nature. Follow the process where horses play a big role in the development of many peoples lives in a collaboration with different schools, habilitations and organisations. Create/implement social activities and logistics of other EU projects. Assist the team in strengthening the community of volunteers and create an international location in the countryside. Accommodation, food and transport arrangements Accomodation: Throughout the duration of the volunteering experience will be funded by the project. Volunteers will be placed in a private house with their own kitchen, bathrooms and showers. There is a common area with TV and Wifi. Outside is a barbeque area. Personal Allowance & Food Budget: Budgets will be allocated every month. Volunteers will be responsible to do their own shopping. Transportation: If the volunteers have a driver's license they will be provided a car for shopping. There are also bikes to use whenever you please. Transportation for big cities are buses/trains. Training during the activity Training and Support: On arrival, volunteers will be supported to conduct a number of orientation visits of the services that Caprifolen operates. Caprifolen will provide training on how to work with/around horses and with the target group -people with disabilities. The Swedish National Agency will provide arrival training. Further support will be offered on a weekly basis through meetings and reviews of activities. Mentors of Caprifolen will offer support through the whole project and process. Participant profile Eligible participants are young people between 18 and 30 years of age. We are seeking a person who would like to live on a horse farm. You should be motivated and willing to work in a dynamic team we different people with or without disabilities, share ideas and take initiative. A background with experience in the social field, horses, theater, dance, gymnastics, circus, horse vaulting is considered an asset. European Solidarity Corps The power of together menu Log in General information Young people Organisations Projects General information Young people Organisations Projects Volunteering in nature - ESC at Landschap De Liereman Belgium BC Landschap De Liereman vzw Oud-Turnhout, Belgium Apply Description Do you love nature and being outdoors? Do you want to make a difference for nature and the local community? Than you are the volunteer we are looking for! The visitor center ‘Landschap De Liereman’ is located near Turnhout, a small city in the north of Belgium. The center is connected to ‘Landschap De Liereman’ one of the oldest recognized nature reserves in Belgium. We stand up for nature and biodiversity and strive to protect open space and the quality of the environment. You will participate in the daily activities of the visitor center and the reserve 'Landschap De Liereman.' This means helping in the bar and kitchen during the opening hours, working in nature, supporting our volunteers with nature conservation and restoration, occasional participation in the care of the semi-wild grazers (horses, ponies and Galloways), assisting with administrative tasks, cleaning, and maintenance, and developing educational activities for kids. Accommodation, food and transport arrangements You will stay in a safe accommodation in and around Turnhout. You will be co-housing with the other ESC volunteers. The accommodation has separate bedrooms and a shared bathroom, kitchen and living room. We offer you a bike to explore the area and reach the visitor center. The bike will also be your main means of transport. You receive pocket money and a food allowance. We also offer lunch posibilities in the visitor center. Don’t worry about travel expenses for the trip. Everything is covered. Training during the activity You will receive on-the-job training from our staff and volunteers. You will be involved in various tasks and projects but also take initiative. The volunteering project also gives you the opportunity to join an on-arrival and mid-term training, organized by the national agency. Participant profile We are looking for volunteers between the ages of 18 and 30 years who love nature and being outdoors, enjoy meeting new people and working as a team with other volunteers, are willing to learn new skills, take initiative, like to think out of the box, have a flexible attitude and are responsible. Do you recognize yourself in this description? Then sign up to volunteer at the visitor center! 60.name: Gleneayre Equestrian Program Working Student
60.text: Working Student Program - Gleneayre Equestrian Program --> Home About What We Do Mission Statement Programs Working Student Program Equine Assisted Learning Veterans Equine Program Corporate Programs Our Team Our Horses Galleries News Events Horse Show & Hunter Derby Partners Support How to Help Sponsor a Horse Sponsor a Child Easy Giving Volunteer Contact Donate Search START TYPING AND PRESS ENTER TO SEARCH Home About What We Do Mission Statement Programs Working Student Program Equine Assisted Learning Veterans Equine Program Corporate Programs Our Team Our Horses Galleries News Events Horse Show & Hunter Derby Partners Support How to Help Sponsor a Horse Sponsor a Child Easy Giving Volunteer Contact Donate Search Mission Statement Programs --> Corporate Programs Veterans Equine Program Working Student Program Equine Assisted Learning (EAL) Our Team WHAT WE DO Mission Statement Programs --> Corporate Programs Veterans Equine Program Working Student Program Equine Assisted Learning (EAL) Our Team Working Student Program Horsemanship doesn’t have to be expensive. Give us your commitment, and we will give you the rest. We welcome qualified students starting at age 10 through their first postsecondary year to apply to our Working Student Program. We look for applicants who may be at risk and love horses and are driven to learn about riding and caring for them. Students must supply their own ASTM/SEI-approved riding helmet. We provide all other equipment and tack. We pair students with their own horse and teach them all aspects of horsemanship. We instruct, monitor, and supervise our students closely. They are responsible for their horses, including grooming and exercising, cleaning stalls and tack, barn management, and limited aspects of supervised veterinary care. Our students work with their horses four days a week, one weekend day, and three days after school. Each student keeps a log of his or her horse’s care. We also encourage our students to participate in 4-H, USEF- and USDF-sanctioned events, clinics, workshops, horse shows, academic competitions, and community service. Our Working Student Program provides a safe space for our students to learn, develop, and grow. Key outcomes include relationship-building skills, personal responsibility, self-confidence, and healthy stress relief. Our Community Partners and Sponsors

Network & News Press Releases Article Share: Apply Now for 2026 USEF Higher Education Equestrian Scholarships by US Equestrian Communications Dept. | 2/4/2026 Lexington, Ky . – US Equestrian is now accepting applications for the  2026 Higher Education Equestrian Scholarships . This scholarship program awards two $2,500 scholarships annually to graduating high school seniors who are committed to advancing their equine knowledge or equestrian experience while in college.  (©Devyn Trethewey/US Equestrian) Meet the 2025 Higher Education Equestrian Scholarship recipients.   The scholarships are available to US Equestrian competing members and subscribers who are current high school seniors and are preparing to enter college full-time in the fall of 2026. Equestrians from all US Equestrian-affiliated breeds and disciplines pursuing any major are encouraged to apply.  Applicants may meet the requirement to continue their involvement with horses during college through equine-related coursework, participation on a college riding team or club, or horse-related internships, jobs, or volunteer work.  Scholarship funds will be issued directly to the recipients’ educational institutions to be applied to their tuition costs.  How to Apply   The application package includes proof of full-time college enrollment, a letter explaining the applicant’s involvement in horse-related activity, a reference letter from an individual in the horse industry, and an essay written by the applicant answering questions about their current and future involvement with equestrian sports.  Click  here  to access the application form and see full application instructions.  All application materials, including essay and reference letters, must be submitted by 11:59 p.m. ET on July 31, 2026. Please direct any questions to Lexie Stovel at  [email protected] .  Related Topics General: Youth Information Member Login Community Building Careers Privacy Legal Site Map Donate USET US Equestrian Contact United States Equestrian Federation 4001 Wing Commander Way Lexington, KY 40511 Call: 859-810-8733 [email protected] Member, United States Olympic & Paralympic Committee © 2026 United States Equestrian Federation

This programme is a rare opportunity for confident, experienced horse lovers to live and work on a prestigious yard, all organised before you leave home. Positions are full-time, paid, and include hands-on work with horses at top-class facilities. You’ll start with 8 nights in Sydney for orientation, giving you time to recover from jet lag, get your admin sorted (including bank accounts, SIM cards and tax numbers), and make friends with other solo travellers. There’s also the option to take on casual city work before or after your stud placement too.   Book Now Price from: £1945 £ US$ € C$ AU$ --> Price guide 15 travellers rated this project 5 15 Scroll for More Trustpilot Step into a pre-arranged paid groom role at one of Australia’s leading stud farms. This programme is a rare opportunity for confident, experienced horse lovers to live and work on a prestigious yard, all organised before you leave home. Positions are full-time, paid, and include hands-on work with horses at top-class facilities. You’ll start with 8 nights in Sydney for orientation, giving you time to recover from jet lag, get your admin sorted (including bank accounts, SIM cards and tax numbers), and make friends with other solo travellers. Duration: 8 days orientation, 3 -6 months work, 1 year support (optional) Dates: Click here for availability calendar Arrival: Sunday Eligibility: Must be eligible for a 417 or 462 visa. Driving licence required. Horse ability: intermediate Age : 18-35 Share with friends... Highlights Guaranteed Paid Job with Horses : Step into a pre-arranged groom position at a top Australian stud or farm Real-World Equine Experience : Boost your CV with hands-on work, professional references, and exposure to elite horse care environments Exclusive 8-Night Sydney Orientation : Settle in with a week of admin support, city exploration, and new travel mates Optional City Work Opportunities : Switch it up later with access to job support in Sydney and beyond 12 Months of Ongoing Support : Stay flexible and confident with expert help available throughout your time in Australia Location - Map Sydney NSW, Australia Description 🐴 Your First Week in Australia Begin your Aussie adventure with a week in sunny Sydney, the perfect way to settle in. Meet like-minded travellers and prepare for your exciting placement working with horses. You’ll enjoy sightseeing, social events, and admin support to get everything sorted before you head off to your stud placement. What’s Included in Your Arrival Package: 🛬 Airport Shuttle from Sydney Airport to your hostel for a smooth landing 🏨 Accommodation included at Wake Up! Hostel – one of Australia’s most highly rated backpacker stays. There will be 6 nights at this hostel and in the middle of the week, a 2 night excursion to Port Stephens to sport dolphins and stay at the beach! 🌆 Sydney Highlights Tour, Welcome Dinner & Rooftop Views of the iconic Opera House 🥳 Group Activities & Nights Out to meet up to 30 solo travellers just like you 💳 Bank Account Setup & Tax File Number assistance 📱 Australian SIM card and help choosing the best plan 📄 CV Review & Job Coaching for future work in the city or beyond 💼 12-Month Job Membership with access to city-based roles after your horse farm placement 📦 12-Month Mail Holding Service for post and packages 💻 Free Wi-Fi, Medicare Guidance & Discounted RSA/White Card Courses 🏡 Help Finding Housing if you choose to work in Sydney later on 💰 Tax Refund & Superannuation Support when your job ends 🐎 Your Paid Horse Job Following your arrival week, you’ll travel to a pre-arranged, full-time groom position at a reputable Australian stud farm. You’ll live on-site, receive training from experienced staff once you arrive, and become part of a busy working yard. These placements typically run for 3–12 months , with accommodation included , allowing you to save money while doing something you love. You’ll be employed directly by the stud farm pre-departure, and we will stay in touch throughout for any support you may need. Need to Know: 🌍 Open to 18–35-year-olds eligible for a Working Holiday Visa (subclass 417 or 462) ⏳ Placements available for a minimum of 3, but up to 12 months , depending on stud requirements 💵 Paid positions: you’ll earn minimum wage or higher , accommodation included 🛏️ Extra nights before/after the intro week can be added for £30 per night 🌾 City job options available after your farm work if you wish to extend your stay With a structured start, a guaranteed paid job, and full support throughout your stay, this is the ultimate way to combine your passion for horses with a new life across the globe in Australia. What Will You Be Doing? Start with a fun and productive 8-night Sydney orientation designed to help you adjust, make friends, and get everything sorted for your working holiday in Australia. 🛬 Join a group of up to 30 solo travellers 📄 Set up your Aussie bank account, SIM card, tax number & Medicare 🌆 Explore world-famous sights like the Sydney Opera House, Harbour Bridge & Bondi Beach 🏨 Stay in one of Sydney’s best-located hostels, right in the heart of the city 🎉 Take part in social events, sightseeing tours, and group activities – including a 2 night stay in Port Stephens to experience sandboarding, dolphin cruising and much more 💼 Gain access to 12 months of job support, including help finding city work later in your visa Once you’re settled and ready, it’s time to head to your paid equine job , typically located within reach of Sydney. 🐴 Start work at a reputable stud or riding facility with professional riders 📍 Most jobs are located in rural or semi-rural areas in New South Wales 📆 Stay with your employer for anywhere from 3 to 12 months 🏠 Accommodation is nearly always provided, so your living costs are low and savings potential is high 💰 After your placement, use your earnings to travel, explore, or take on city work with continued support from the Sydney team You’ll finish your first week in Australia with everything you need to hit the ground running; friends, confidence, admin sorted, and a job already confirmed. Accommodation Your First Week in Sydney You’ll kick off your Australian adventure with 8 nights in a premium hostel right in the heart of downtown Sydney. This is in walking distance to all the major sights, cafes, bars, and beaches. Expect vibrant social vibes and top-notch facilities designed for travellers: ✔️ 6–10 bed dorm in a centrally located hostel ✔️ Super-fast Wi-Fi, onsite café, bar & games room ✔️ Daily social activities and travel desk support ✔️ Clean, safe, and ideal for meeting other backpackers ✔️ Perfect base to explore Bondi, the Harbour, and beyond (your two night stay in Port Stephens – a couple of hours north of Sydney – is in beautiful cabin style dorm rooms, 5 minutes from the beach) At Your Groom Job Once you move into your equine role, you’ll be provided with high-quality onsite private room accommodation included as part of your wage. Most placements include: ✔️ A shared house on or very near the property ✔️ Kitchen facilities for self-catering ✔️ Close proximity to horses, stables, and open countryside ✔️ A relaxed, rural lifestyle, a complete change of pace from the city From buzzing Sydney to peaceful paddocks, you’ll experience the best of both worlds during your time in Australia. Travel arrangements Airport: SYD Sydney NSW Arrival day : Sunday – a complimentary pre-orientation night will be booked for you for Sunday night Transfer details: When you arrive at the airport you will be given a shuttle voucher for a bus to transport you to central Sydney. Departure day: Monday. Intermediate horse handling skills needed for safety but you don’t have to be the best rider in the world! This opportunity is designed for confident, experienced horse riders and handlers looking to take their equine skills to the next level while earning money abroad. Whether you’ve worked in stables, competed, or cared for horses recreationally, this programme gives you the chance to expand your hands-on experience in a professional, fast-paced stud environment. Our breeding industry is the biggest after America, meaning thoroughbreds make a huge contribution to the regional economy, supporting thousands of jobs directly, as well as many more in related areas such as horse transport, fencing, fodder and insurance. Thoroughbred Breeders Australia is committed to helping the industry find the next generation of workers and dedicates significant time and resources to finding enthusiastic people to take up roles on studs across the country. Register Your Interest The FAST TRACK program Thoroughbred breeders Australia has launched the Fast Track program to create a pathway for talented horse people to enter our industry. If you are passionate about horses this is an incredible opportunity: you will be employed and mentored on a respected stud farm; gain a formal qualification, plus have the opportunity to learn from leaders in the global horse industry. The 12 month program provides an accelerated introduction and learning experience that will help Fast Track graduates progress quickly in careers in the breeding industry. We believe that trainees will gain an unmatched introduction in all aspects of the thoroughbred industry, allowing them to kick-start a career that offers a world of possibilities. This opportunity is aimed at anybody who has a passion for horses, no matter your equine background. If you enjoy an active lifestyle, working outdoors in a social and fast-paced environment, Fast Track could be the start of an exciting new career. 2020 Fast Track intake. Founded in 1994, Work to Ride (WTR) is a 501 c3, nonprofit community-based prevention program that aids under-resourced urban youth through constructive activities centered on horsemanship, equine sports and education. The program is housed at Chamounix Stables located in Fairmount Park, Philadelphia. Work to Ride has achieved national recognition, including HBO Real Sports and featured in Sports Illustrated and various other regional, national and international media outlets. WTR Application Volunteer Form REAL CHANGE Stories of Hope. My name is Kareem Rosser, and I am a graduate of the Work to Ride program. I grew up in West Philadelphia, in a neighborhood known as “The Bottom”, where going to jail or getting shot is just as likely for young men as high school graduation. One day while riding bicycles in Fairmount Park, my older brothers stumbled upon a run-down barn with horses. That day they met Work to Ride’s founder, Lezlie Hiner, who had spotted the boys and told them if they stayed in school, she would teach them how to ride. When I found out where my brothers had been going after school, I begged them to take me along! It was 2000 and… Continue Reading × Kareem Rosser ​​My name is Kareem Rosser, and I am a graduate of the Work to Ride program. I grew up in West Philadelphia, in a neighborhood known as “The Bottom”, where going to jail or getting shot is just as likely for young men as high school graduation. One day while riding bicycles in Fairmount Park, my older brothers stumbled upon a run-down barn with horses. That day they met Work to Ride’s founder, Lezlie Hiner, who had spotted the boys and told them if they stayed in school, she would teach them how to ride. When I found out where my brothers had been going after school, I begged them to take me along! It was 2000 and I was 8 years old when I joined the Work to Ride program. In 2011, I captained the Work to Ride Polo Team, which was the first-ever all African American team to win the National Interscholastic Polo Championship. In 2015, I led my Colorado State University Polo Team to the National Intercollegiate Polo Championship and was named the Intercollegiate Polo Player of the Year. In 2016, I graduated from Colorado State University with a degree in economics. Through Work to Ride, I was provided with educational and equestrian opportunities I would not have had otherwise as a kid growing up in West Philadelphia. Today, I reside in Philadelphia and work in finance. I currently serve on the executive committee of Work to Ride’s board of directors. My hope is to expand the program so it allows more at-risk youth the opportunities that I was given. Together , we can make a difference. Changing lives, one rider at a time. DONATE DONATE All manners of support benefit the WTR program, whether its stomping divots at the Philadelphia Polo Classic, contributing to our building fund, or supporting a child at summer camp will all help positively impact the lives of Philadelphia urban youth. Courses Foundation Course All I’m New to Racing Diploma Courses Stable Staff Courses Jockey Short Courses Management Pony Racing Trainers Administration Courses Other International Riders Rider Development Pathway Foundation Course News Donate Apply Now News New course for 16-18 year olds Published on 11th February 2025 We’re delighted to launch a new programme for 16-18 year olds looking to turn their passion for horses into an equestrian career. The new Level 2 Qualification in Racehorse Care and Exercise is a two-year, non-residential course which will launch this September and will equip students with the skills and confidence to pursue a career in one of the most rewarding equestrian sports in the country. Based at the world-class facilities of the British Racing School in Newmarket, Suffolk, students will attend during term time where they will gain hands on experience working with and riding the 70 racehorses that live at the school. The course will also provide students with an understanding of racehorse health and welfare, stable management and racing knowledge, preparing them for a rewarding career at a racing yard. Andrew Braithwaite, Chief Executive of the British Racing School said, “We are excited to offer this new qualification for young people aged 16-18 who are looking to pursue a career with horses. The new course will give students the skills and experience they need to excel in the sport, whilst allowing them to continue to live at home and attend the school on a day basis. With an emphasis on practical skills and real-world application, the Level 2 course is designed to support the next generation of racing professionals and help them become a key part of this dynamic industry.” In the first year, students will work towards their Level 1 qualification before going on to either complete their Level 2 at the school or an apprenticeship at a racing yard. The course complements other entry-level stable staff courses offered by the British Racing School, including the Foundation Course which has supported thousands of young people into the sport over the past 40 years. Network & News Press Releases Article Share: US Equestrian Announces 2025 USEF Horsemastership Training Series by US Equestrian Communications Dept. | 11/1/2024 Lexington, Ky. - US Equestrian is pleased to announce that the 2025 USEF Horsemastership Training Series will take place January 2-5, 2025, at the Adequan® Global Dressage Festival showgrounds in Wellington, Fla. The Horsemastership Training Series is designed to identify and develop the next generation of top U.S. Show Jumping team athletes and includes mounted and unmounted sessions with top professionals. This year’s instructors for the mounted sessions are Anne Kursinski, Beezie Madden, and McLain Ward. In principle, the program is open to six (6) US Citizens ages 16-21, who are currently successful in competition at 1.40m or above. The intimate class size will offer the athletes the opportunity for an engaging, and immersive experience. This tailored approach allows for more personalized instruction and greater interaction with the clinicians and their peers. ©US Equestrian Athletes are invited to participate in the 2025 Horsemastership Training Series based on their results at US Jumping Pathway Programs, along with wild card athletes. Athletes from the pathway programs include Individual gold medalists of the FEI North American Junior and Young Rider Championships, Platinum Performance/USEF Talent Search Finals East/West, and Turnham Greem/USEF Junior Jumper National Championships. Individuals interested in submitting a wild card application can go to their  Athlete Dashboard  on the US Equestrian website under online applications year 2025 to apply. All applications are due by November 18, 2024. Athletes wishing to participate in the Training Series must have completed a minimum of three (3) courses held at 1.40m or higher with a score of zero (0) faults within six (6) months of the close of application for the event. Athletes are required to bring a horse for the training series. Horses must be very fit and proficient at 1.40m or higher over open water. Athletes are strongly encouraged to participate with their own horse, limiting borrowed horses. The Jumping Development and Youth Chefs d’Equipe will review the applicants and base their evaluations on the following criteria:   An athlete’s historical results in jumper competitions held within the previous year at USEF and FEI sanctioned events and results from 2024 U.S. Jumping Pathway Programs. Emphasis will be placed on results from competitions held at 1.40m and above, and the competitiveness of the competition. In addition, the athlete’s ability to compete without incurring jumping and/or time penalties at the highest level of course difficulty, as well as the athlete’s record of having done so on a consistent basis, will be considered.  An athlete’s perceived potential to successfully contribute to future US Team Competitions.    For more information about the USEF Horsemastership Training Series, click here .    Follow US Equestrian  Stay up to date on the U.S. Jumping Team by following USA Jumping on Facebook and Instagram . Current trainees Trainees 1st Year 2025 – 2027 Calendar Trainees 2nd Year 2024 – 2026 Calendar Graduates Graduates Recruit a Graduate Conferences Growing a Legacy Alumni Community Portal News Latest News Podcast Contact us A leader takes people where they want to go. A great leader takes people where they ought to be Learn More Godolphin Flying Start is a two year, full time Management and Leadership scholarship dedicated to shaping the future of the global Thoroughbred industry. An unparalleled training experience Our mission is to recruit 12 talented individuals from around the world every year, offering unparalleled training and experience to cultivate visionary leaders. Through this fully funded scholarship, we aim to drive the long-term success of the industry, empowering graduates to make a lasting impact in both established and emerging fields. LEARN MORE 02 Years 05 Countries 12 Trainees 248 Global graduate alumni network 50 % Of graduates in management positions or running their own business. What we stand for Excellence We challenge and support each other to learn and to excel in everything we do. Growth & Development We believe in building character through self-awareness, commitment and continuous growth. We willingly teach and mentor others. Enhancement of the Industry We embrace innovation and are dedicated to improving the accessibility and well-being of our industry. We contribute to the positive public perception of horseracing by building a diverse, equal and inclusive community with an open and international perspective. Leadership & Integrity We believe in the power of people to lead change and to achieve ambitious goals. We show appreciation for what we have and pride in what we do. Commitment to the Thoroughbred We commit to developing a deep and broad knowledge of the horse and providing exceptional care, with a focus on the lifelong welfare of the Thoroughbred. LEARN MORE What our graduates say: When I started Flying Start I had a vague idea about running my own business one day. The programme and all the experiences during the two years gave me confidence and courage to make my dreams become reality. Laura Vanska Racehorse Trainer, France Godolphin Flying Start gives you unmatched global industry knowledge and experience, along with an invaluable international network. Whether at a sale in Kentucky or a racecourse in Australia, you’re likely to encounter a familiar face from the Flying Start community – it’s a network that spans every corner of the racing world. Vicky Leonard Founder and Managing Director, Kick Collective Godolphin Flying Start has had an immeasurable influence on my career. The two-year programme itself was incredible, but as time passes, you realise that the connections between all the graduates is the most special aspect of it. There are now over 230 graduates of the programme, and it is an incredible network of friends and colleagues. Cathal Beale CEO, Irish National Stud and Gardens The impact of the Godolphin Flying Start program on both my personal and professional life has been transformative. My successes can be directly traced back to the opportunities and experiences gained through this remarkable scholarship. It’s a privilege to be part of such a powerful network, and I take great joy in connecting with the next generation of graduates as they embark on their own journeys and make the most of all the programme has to offer. Arvin Chadee Nominations Manager at Darley NSW Australia Godolphin Flying Start was a game-changer for me. Wanamaker’s actually started as my final business plan for the course, and the program gave me the confidence and tools to turn that idea into a reality. The network I built through Flying Start opened doors I couldn’t have imagined, including leading to our partnership with Inglis. I wouldn’t be where I am today without the skills and connections I gained through the program. Liza Hendriks CEO at Inglis Digital USA Even years after graduating from Godolphin Flying Start, I’m reminded daily of how much the program has given me. A placement with Thoroughbred Daily News led to a full-time role that I’ve grown in over the past seven years. The course provided me with a deep understanding of the global industry and connected me with life-long friends, my husband, and a network of professionals worldwide. Alayna Cullen Birkett Business Development Manager at TDN Godolphin Flying Start has given me unique global view in an ever-growing international market. I feel very fortunate to know that in each jurisdiction whether it is a graduate or someone we all met along the way, there will be opportunity, connection or friend to catch up with. The amazing thing is that this multiplies each year after the programme. Henry Morshead Assistant Trainer, George Boughey Racing Global A global experience. The Godolphin International Thoroughbred Leadership Programme is a two-year, full-time management course combining lectures, visits, and hands-on experience. With continual assessment, externships, and self-study, it partners with global universities to deliver certified business and equine modules. Across a two-year period, trainees live and learn in five major racing jurisdictions outlined below. LEARN MORE IRELAND ( PHASE 1) Kildare, Ireland 10 weeks – Goffs Orby Yearling Sale – Race meetings at The Curragh and Leopardstown – Practical experience with yearlings at Kildangan Stud – Equine Anatomy, Physiology & Reproduction at UCD – Multimedia, Communication and Personal Development modules UNITED KINGDOM Newmarket, UK 8 weeks – Race meetings at Newmarket and Chelmsford – Tattersalls Breeding Stock Sale – Office experience at Dalham Hall Stud – Legal, Bloodstock Insurance, Accounting, Racing Form and Media Skills modules.   USA Kentucky, USA 26 weeks – Race meetings at Keeneland and Churchill Downs – Practical experience on breeding farms – Equine Nutrition at University of Kentucky – Farriery & Racing Officials Programme – Public Speaking – Legal, Finance and Pedigree Analysis – Four-week externship AUSTRALIA NSW, Australia 22 weeks – Race meetings at Sydney and Melbourne – Practical experience on breeding farms – Leadership module at Australian Graduate School of Management – Business Management and Bloodstock modules – Magic Millions Gold Coast Yearling Sale – Eight-week externship DUBAI Dubai, UAE 3 weeks – Race meetings at Meydan, Jebel Ali and Abu Dhabi Turf Club – Visits to Dubai-based trainers – Customer discovery workshops – Cultural visits and experiences IRELAND (PHASE 2) Kildare, Ireland 18 weeks – Race meetings at Naas, Punchestown and Dundalk – Career Mentoring – Professional Development – Entrepreneurship module at UCD Michael Smurfit Business School Business Management – Pitching business ideas – Industry Structure, Funding and Administration Four-week externship How to apply ? Are you who we are looking for? Are you seeking to develop excellent management and leadership training and a career in the Thoroughbred Industry? Do you have excellent communication skills and in-depth knowledge and passion for horse racing? Are you someone who enjoys a rigorous academic program and multi-cultural experiences? Do you have competent horse handling skills?   LEARN MORE Our partners: Godolphin Flying Start is fortunate to be supported by a large number of leading industry businesses and organisations including: Latest News 2026 – 2028 Trainees Announced June 4, 2026 READ MORE Orlaith Nangle Joins Tattersalls as Marketing Executive March 18, 2026 READ MORE Chris Farrell appointed as Operations & Digital Communications Executive March 16, 2026 READ MORE The BIG Questions How much does Godolphin Flying Start cost? Godolphin Flying Start is a fully-funded two-year scholarship. Apart from personal expenses, trainees should incur no financial burden throughout the duration of the course. This allows them to focus fully on their education, practical experience, and career development in the industry. What is included in the scholarship? Godolphin Flying Start covers all major expenses for trainees which include the following: All course fees Accommodation Local transport International health insurance Monthly living allowance Flight allowances Visa and immigration costs Do I need a degree to apply? No, not necessarily. While most applicants do hold degrees, it is not the only eligibility criteria. The programme typically seeks applicants with an NFQ Level 8 qualification, equating to a bachelor’s degree. However, there are alternate pathways: NFQ Level 7 Qualification with Industry Experience: If you hold an NFQ Level 7 at honours level and have amassed three to five years of full-time relevant experience in the Thoroughbred industry post-graduation, you’re a potential candidate. NFQ Level 6 Qualification with Extensive Experience: For those with a Level 6 qualification at honours level coupled with at least 10 years of full-time relevant industry employment, including roles of responsibility, the doors to Godolphin Flying Start are open. Do I have to be from a racing background to apply? No, Godolphin Flying Start welcomes all. The world of thoroughbred racing and breeding is often perceived as an exclusive realm, steeped in tradition and, more often than not, accessible mostly to those born into it. However, the Godolphin Flying Start programme breaks down these barriers, offering a unique opportunity that transcends family backgrounds and traditional entry points into the racing world. The thoroughbred industry, like any other, thrives on diversity and fresh perspectives. Bringing in talent from various backgrounds can introduce new ideas, strategies, and outlooks that can propel the industry forward. Godolphin Flying Start recognizes this potential and actively encourages applicants from all walks of life. Do I need to be competent around horses? Yes, you do need to have a basic level of competency handling horses to apply for Godolphin Flying Start. Once selected for interview you will be asked to provide a video demonstrating horsemanship competency in the following areas: Tacking up and lunging a horse. Tacking up and putting a horse on a walker. Grooming and presenting a mare, yearling or foal for inspection. Riding a quiet horse in an enclosed area. Do I need to have worked in the Thoroughbred industry? Yes, you need at least one year in paid, full-time, hands-on employment in the Thoroughbred industry to meet the application criteria for Godolphin Flying Start. Contact Us If you’re thinking of applying for Godolphin Flying Start but have more questions to ask, please feel free to get in touch with a member of our team. Participants then go on to paid placements which are usually eight weeks but, in some cases, may be longer. Qualification TBC Duration 8 Weeks – 1 Year Study type Full-time, Residential, Various Course overview This unique Programme starts at the beginning of July with a FREE 2 week residential induction course in Newmarket, a market town in the county of Suffolk, often referred to as the Home of British Horseracing. The course is packed with lectures, tours and visits. Following this course, is a paid (on or above the National Living Wage or London Living Wage if the placement is based in London) industry placement starting immediately and lasting for at least 8 weeks (some are longer depending on the business need). Here you can apply the knowledge learnt from the 2 week course to excel in your selected placement. Take a look at the placements that are on offer in 2025. There are usually placements in Marketing, Digital, Journalism and Sales, to name a few. Placements are subject to change and when you apply, you will be asked to submit your preferred area of interest and your career aspirations. Approximately 16 to 20 places are available each year, in a variety of organisations, in locations across the UK. The programme provides an invaluable source of talent to the industry. Many of the programme’s alumni have established careers in a wide range of roles; management positions at racecourses, broadcasters, the trade press, bloodstock sales and also at the British Horseracing Authority. Eligibility This is an early careers programme suitable for candidates finishing higher education or those that have equivalent skills and capabilities, looking to develop a career in either sport or the horseracing industry. Candidates who are completing a degree or higher apprenticeship should be in their final year or have finished their studying. For further information, take a look at the FAQs What will you learn This Programme offers approximately 16 – 20 placements each year, in a variety of organisations, in locations across the UK and are paid on or above the Living Wage. Placements differ year on year but usually include organisations such as the Racecourse Association, The Racing Post, Weatherbys, Thoroughbred Breeders Association, Jockey Club Racecourses and Great British Racing. Monday, November 10, 2025 Contact: Shannon Luce 859.224.2716 Applications Open for The Jockey Club�s Internship Program and Academic Scholarships Applications for The Jockey Club’s paid internship program for college students and recent college graduates and its five academic scholarships opened today. The internship program will accommodate up to three interns and is now available in the summer or fall based on the intern’s preference. Interns will gain exposure to all companies and departments within the organization, and they will have the opportunity to spend additional time in areas that they find to be of particular interest. The program will last eight weeks. The internships will take place in The Jockey Club’s office located in Lexington, Ky. For more information and to view the application, interested candidates should visit bit.ly/tjcinternships . Applications are being accepted through January 5, 2026. Selected interns will be notified in February 2026. The Jockey Club scholarships will apply to the 2026-2027 academic year and are as follows:   The Jockey Club Scholarship ($15,000; $7,500 per semester) is for those enrolled full time as an undergraduate or graduate student at a college or university in academic pursuit of majors for future employment in the equine industry. Open to any equine breed or discipline; preference will be given to candidates with the expressed desire to secure employment in the Thoroughbred industry. The Jockey Club Advancement of Women in Racing Scholarship ($20,000; $10,000 per semester) is open to women pursuing a career in the equine industry who are enrolled full time as an undergraduate student at a college or university. Open to any equine breed or discipline; preference will be given to candidates with the expressed desire to secure employment in the Thoroughbred industry. The Jockey Club Vision Scholarship ($20,000; $10,000 per semester) is open to students from a minority racial or ethnic group who are pursuing a career in the equine industry. Applicants must be enrolled full time as an undergraduate student at a college or university. Open to any equine breed or discipline; preference will be given to candidates with the expressed desire to secure employment in the Thoroughbred industry. The Jockey Club Nancy C. Kelly Benevolence Scholarship ($15,000; $7,500 per semester) is a need-based award for Thoroughbred racing farm or backstretch workers and their families who have experienced financial hardship to enable attendance at a full-time program at a college, university, or trade program. Applicants are not required to be pursuing a career in the equine or Thoroughbred industries. The Jockey Club Jack Goodman Scholarship ($6,000; $3,000 per semester) is open to students enrolled in the University of Arizona’s Race Track Industry Program (RTIP). Goodman was a resident of Tucson, a longtime member of The Jockey Club, and one of three founders of the RTIP. Applications for all five scholarships are open through January 5, 2026. Click here ( bit.ly/tjcscholarshipsapps ) for more information and links to applications for the scholarships . The recipients of each scholarship will be announced in the spring of 2026 in advance of the initial distribution of funds for the fall 2026 semester. The Jockey Club, founded in 1894 and dedicated to the improvement of Thoroughbred breeding and racing, is the breed registry for Thoroughbreds in the U.S., Canada, and Puerto Rico. In fulfillment of its mission, The Jockey Club provides support and leadership on a wide range of important industry initiatives, and directly or through subsidiaries, it serves the information and technology needs of the sport of Thoroughbred racing. Among its many supported initiatives are America’s Best Racing ( americasbestracing.net ), OwnerView ( ownerview.com ), and the Thoroughbred Incentive Program ( tjctip.com ). 74.name: BridgeUP GiddyUP (Memphis) — Free Year-Round Program
74.text: BridgeUP GiddyUP – The Helen Gurley Brown Foundation Home About Who was Helen Gurley Brown? Our History Our Team What We Fund Our Philosophy Empowering Youth Empowering Women Empowering Innovation Contact Blog Search BridgeUP GiddyUP What is BridgeUP GiddyUP? BridgeUP GiddyUP provides youth with a unique track to personal development through the medium of horseback riding. The program teaches horsemanship and animal husbandry, and provides a rigorous skills curriculum for students which adhere to the European standards. In addition to athletic training and skill development, students receive holistic personal and social development skills. Fitness testing and health education help improve nutrition, coordination, balance, and flexibility. Group activities and community engagement provide opportunities for students to develop critical social skills including communication, responsibility, teamwork, and problem-solving. THE PROBLEM & PARADIGM Why horseback riding? Launched in 2021, BridgeUP GiddyUP was created to help bridge horsemanship and economic gaps within the equestrian community. The sport of horseback riding has historically been overwhelmingly high-income — the average yearly household income for members of the United States Equestrian Federation is $185,000. The cost of purchasing and maintaining a horse, much less competing, is prohibitive to most families. BridgeUP GiddyUP is therefore completely free of charge to those accepted, and available to students in the Whitehaven community, located in Memphis Tennessee, between the ages of 11-18. Accepted students receive, at minimum, 2 horseback riding lessons per week, transportation to the Southern Blues Equestrian Center, as well as healthy meals and complementary activities including mentorship from leaders in the broader Memphis community. BridgeUP GiddyUP seeks to guide young people into a future in which they can take the skills they learn through horse sport into any future career path, within the “horse world” and beyond it. THE APPROACH Galloping Ahead One of the most biggest impediments to greater success for most riders is the event-based model for progression in the traditional equestrian world. This model works against students with the least resources as it requires constant travel, ever-accumulating horse show costs, and an immense time commitment. BridgeUP GiddyUP provides an alternative path to success for under-resourced riders through rigorous, merit-based testing events to augment conventional competitions as the milestones to an individual’s success. These level-scaling, merit-testing events are based on classical riding standards which are not only time tested but have been evolved through the history of horsemanship to the current best practices for animal welfare and therefore long-term sustainable horse-human partnerships. Testing events are held as warranted by students’ progress and readiness to progress to the next level. At the conclusion of each program year, participants have the opportunity to showcase their equestrian skills in a major competition format, which serves to celebrate their achievements as well as present equestrian sport to a much broader audience in an accessible and exciting production.    But GiddyUP is about more than just learning how to ride. Working with horses teaches students work ethic, confidence, teamwork, responsibility, health science, and so many other lessons that are vital to the development of the whole rider. And of course – it’s fun! GiddyUP Success so far As of 2024, over the course of its three years of operation, BridgeUP GiddyUP has already started to see the impacts of horse sport on our cohorts of student riders and, through partnership with local horse shows, have begun taking steps to impact the wider equestrian ecosystem in Memphis and beyond. Riders report feeling more confident, healthy, and decisive, as well as better able to work with their peers. Self-reporting from both riders and their parents reflect improved academic performance and increased awareness of and desire to attend a four-year college. And we are only at the beginning of what BridgeUP GiddyUP can do! As riders progress through middles and high school, the program will grow and progress with them, helping ensure that their futures, and the future of horse sport, are full of diversity, accessibility, possibility, and magic! 75.name: Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Breeding Management Course
75.text: Thoroughbred Breeding Management | Irish National Stud, Kildare Skip to main content Plan your visit Events Irish Racehorse Experience Tours Stud Gardens Dining Kids Zone Shop Education Thoroughbred Breeding Management School Tours Venue Hire Indoor Stud Services Syndicates Boarding Sales Preparation Foaling Stallions Shop Book Tickets Home Thoroughbred Breeding Management Thoroughbred Breeding Management Applications are now open for 2026. The six-month residential course which commences in January each year runs for the duration of the breeding season and the small group of students form friendships and professional contacts which are often maintained for a lifetime. The core aim of this programme is to equip learners with the knowledge, skills and competence required to perform effectively in responsible positions in the thoroughbred industry. Programme details The programme seeks to equip learners to carry out a range of practical and managerial activities in equine enterprises and, as a result, to enable them to apply managerial principles in an evolving equine industry. Throughout the programme, the learner will undertake a number of assessment activities comprising continuous assessment, practical skills demonstration, projects and final examinations. A particular strength of the programme is the emphasis place on applied and practical skills which are offered through work-based learning. The aim is to ensure graduates have a practical and comprehensive understanding of the thoroughbred industry. The Irish National Stud is the only provider in the country offering a dedicated programme in Thoroughbred Breeding which revolves around the development of a range of transferable skills. Students also develop in-depth knowledge of the global thoroughbred industry through all stages of the programme. Accreditation The Certificate of Thoroughbred Breeding Management is accredited by the South East Technological University. Read more Applications Applications must be submitted online and accompanied by; Current Curriculum Vitae/Resume Cover Letter Two references written on letterhead and/or have the appropriate contact information listed (referee name, company or business, mailing address, phone number and email address) and must be signed Reference should address the applicant’s suitability for the course including any academic achievement, horse-handling experience and personal character Referees may receive a phone call or email from the Irish National Stud to verify the recommendation. Please make sure your references are aware of this English language proficiency certificate Original transcripts of exam results / certificates Applications close October 1st each year. Applicants are notified by email if they have or have not been successful. Applicants may be interviewed in person or via video conferencing   Syllabus The practical aspects of the breeding industry are demonstrated as students rotate around the yards on a weekly basis, gaining knowledge and skills from spending time in the stallion yard, foaling unit, yearling division and stud office. Students attend lectures given by the stud personnel and those involved in the bloodstock industry including vets, farriers, bloodstock agents, etc. and cover a variety of topics including Equine Anatomy & Physiology, Equine Breeding, Equine Nutrition, Bloodstock Management, Equine Business, Pedigree Analysis and Personal & Professional Development. In the latter part of the course, visits are arranged to other stud farms, racing stables, sales companies, etc, and students are encouraged to interact with their personnel to gain a deeper knowledge and understanding of each business area. Entry Requirements and Fees Entry Requirements Applicants must be 18 years or older at time of application Applicants must have obtained an Irish Leaving Certificate or equivalent state examination. Previous practical Thoroughbred experience or knowledge is desirable Applicants for whom English is not their first language must include an IELTS result certificate (academic test) with their application. The required level for selection for the Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Breeding Course is IELTS 5 Fees 2026 Fees EU and UK Nationals: €5,500 International Fees: €7,500 Fees include shared-room accommodation, meals, uniform, tuition, lecture materials and industry site visits Alumni Gallery The Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Alumni is a niche community of over twelve hundred people dispersed throughout the world who share a passion for the thoroughbred industry. Many friendships and relationships were made during the past fifty years and we would like to foster and maintain the INS connection. New address or email? Please keep us updated so that we can send you updates, news and events relevant to you. APPLICATIONS The application process for the 2026 course is now open. Apply Now What the graduates say “The experience & knowledge gained on the Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Breeding Course is unparalleled. The friendships & contacts made along the way have provided new horizons & will prove hugely beneficial for my career progression. Apart from the theoretical lessons & visits to leading stud farms, the hands-on training proved invaluable & all combined to enhance my passion for the Thoroughbred Industry.” Anna Cahill Class of 2022 “When it came to gaining International Thoroughbred experience, the Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Breeding Course was the perfect fit for me. If your goal is to develop and grow as an industry professional, the skills you will learn ad the doors that will be opened, this course will give you every opportunity to do just that.” Alex Sausville Class of 2022 “Having come from a family deeply routed in horse racing, the Irish National Stud course was a given for me. During my 6 months I got to expand my practical and educational knowledge of the Thoroughbred Industry. I not only gained in-depth practical experience of thoroughbred care but I gained valuable friends and connections who I will remain in contact for life” Andrew Buick Class of 2022 “The Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Breeding Course was a fantastic and very enjoyable experience. The course gave me the opportunity to meet some of the industry’s biggest leaders along with making new friends for life. I learned a lot from Irish National Stud staff, vets and guest lectures. It is a well-structured and respected course that has given me a platform to continue working in the thoroughbred industry.” Basil Brindley Class of 2016 “The Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Breeding Course presented the biggest opportunity in my career to date. Working on a world-renowned Stud Farm of the highest quality on a daily basis, attending guest lectures from industry leaders, trips and visits to places such as Bansha House and Jim Bolger’s Glebe House were highlights. I made many connections and friends for life while studying on the course. I would highly recommend the course to anyone looking to further their knowledge of the Thoroughbred industry.” Conor Hyland, Ireland Class of 2020 “The Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Breeding Course is a once in a lifetime experience. It brings together people with all different backgrounds and experiences in an environment in which they can interact, and learn from each other. The breeding course provided me with numerous skills and knowledge that have helped me to start a career in the thoroughbred industry. During my time in Ireland I had the opportunity to visit prominent studs, attend countless racing events, and connect with many of the leaders in this industry. The relationships I made during this time are invaluable.” Sarah Collings, Canada Class of 2020 “I would recommend the course to anyone hoping to make a career in the breeding industry. The combination of lectures, yard work, and guest speakers along with farm and yard visits gives a wonderful overview of the breeding and racing industries. We got a great insight into the mechanics of a large stud farm with a good balance of theory and practical. Working and living with a group of like-minded people, I made some great friends on the course from all over the world which will no doubt benefit us going forward in our careers in the industry.” Nick Cope, Ireland Class of 2019 Graduates CLARE MANNING Boherguy Stud NICK COPE Manager, Stanley Lodge Stud JOE CALLAN Head of bloodstock & sales, Whitsbury Manor Stud PAUL CURRAN Bloodstock, BBA Ireland Alumni The Irish National Stud Thoroughbred Alumni is a niche community of over twelve hundred people dispersed throughout the world who share a passion for the thoroughbred industry. 76.name: Gerry Dilger Equine Scholarship — Irish National Stud
76.text: Irish National Stud Scholarship Find us on social media +1 (859) 621-8062 info@gerrydilgerequine.com Home About News Applications Irish National Stud Scholarship Kentucky Thoroughbred Farm Experience Scholarship Irish National Stud for an Irish Citizen Contact Us Irish National Stud Scholarship 2027 "Gerry took the time to teach me many skills in my early days at Dromoland, but it was Gerry’s willingness to help someone starting out that left a lasting impression on me.  Gerry encouraged me to pursue my interest in nutrition and was hugely supportive when I commenced work in this field." -  Joanne Hurley M.Agr. SC, (Dromoland Farm 1999 – 2002)   Irish Country Manager, GAIN Equine Nutrition, Ireland   Gerry Dilger Equine Scholarship Foundation Irish National Stud Scholarship 2027 In Memory of Gerry Dilger   The Gerry Dilger Equine Scholarship was established in memory of Gerry Dilger (1958-2020), by his family and closest friends. Gerry was a native of Co. Clare, Ireland, and came to America at the young age of 19, to pursue his passion in the horse industry. In 1994, he established Dromoland Farm in Lexington, Kentucky, and had a successful career as a horseman, breeding and consigning thoroughbred horses. Over the next 26 years, he mentored young people who had the same dreams as he did. He took in over 65 young people during his career as his own, helping them gain hands-on experience, sharing knowledge and skills to be successful, and teaching them how to be a respected horseman. He instilled in them the values he had himself; hard work, honesty, expertise, kindness, generosity, loyalty, and above all, a good spirit. Gerry was loved and well- respected in the horse community, and we hope to honor him by continuing his legacy helping young people with their careers. Gerry Dilger's Irish National Stud Class of 1977 Back Row L-R : D. Magee, INS; Bernard McCormack, Ireland; Patrick Kennedy, Ireland; Placement student, John Hand, Ireland; Pierric Rouxel, France; Patrick Lenihan, Ireland; Michael Osborne, INS Middle Row L-R : Robert Lyons, Ireland; Patrick Martin, Ireland; Antonia Feeney, Ireland; Redmond Cunningham, Ireland; Niamh Mullen, Ireland; Andrew Marsh, Ireland; David Brophy, Ireland; Joseph O'Donoghue, Ireland Front Row L-R : Timothy O'Brien, Ireland; Martina Monaghan, INS; Gerard O'Brien, Ireland; Fintan Mc Manus, Ireland; Mary Ellen Trotter, Canada; Gerard Dilger, Ireland; Maurice Burns, Ireland; Kenneth Bourke, Ireland Eligibility Requirements: Currently working on a Kentucky horse farm with multiple years of experience,  OR currently enrolled as a 2026 KEMI spring intern, or must have completed the program in fall of last year Resident of the US Bachelor’s degree in equine science preferred, but not required Application and information due July 1, 2026    This Scholarship Provides: Airfare for student to travel to Ireland, and home at completion of program. Ticket must be economy/coach class, maximum amount $1500. Must provide proof of payment for reimbursement. Fully paid tuition (approx. $9,000) for the Irish National Stud in Kildare, Ireland, including room and board. Travel/Medical insurance policy while in Ireland. Maximum $500. Download PDF --> 0"> Please enter all of the required fields. 1">  Previous Next  “There are so many of us that have been through Dromoland at some stage in our careers and almost without fail - everyone will say it’s some of their fondest memories working for Gerry. I wouldn’t be doing what I am for a living if it hadn’t been for him. I suspect that’s true of a lot of us.” Killian McStay (Dromoland Farm 2011-2013) Manager Oaklawn Stud Ireland “Gerry was not only a mentor but a friend to many. He made me feel right at home when I landed at Dromoland in 2016. He was a true gentleman. He had such a love for horses and a passion for teaching young people. I personally learned so much while I was at Dromoland Farm. I can safely say I was one of the lucky ones to have known and worked with Gerry Dilger. Thank you Gerry for the experience and the most memorable year of my life.” Laura Bennett (2016) Yard foreman at Irish National Stud “The day I walked into Dromoland Farm was a very lucky one! I certainly would not be where I am today without Gerry’s help and encouragement over the five years that I spent there. He had such a natural eye for a horse, it was impossible not to learn just by being around him. Even after I left Dromoland and came home to Ireland Gerry got a great kick out of any success I had. A great mentor and a great friend.” Paula Flannery (1998 - 2003) “It’s hard to put into few words what impact Gerry had on me and my career journey in the business. I think everyone who met through Dromoland Farm all have an unbreakable bond that we all formed through Gerry. Not only did I admire him and everything that he achieved, but respected him so much as a person. Working for Gerry was the best thing I ever did. He believed in me and my ability and gave me so many opportunities to develop and become the confident horse woman I am today. He encouraged and taught me and everyone who worked for him. He thrived on seeing individuals do the best that they could and the love he had for the horse was undeniable. I learned so much from him and he will forever be my greatest mentor.” Erin Rogers (Dromoland Farm 2017-2020) Farm Manager, Norevale Farm. “Having started part time during the summer of 1999 at Dromoland Farm and worked the September sale, I witnessed Gerry’s first “million dollar baby” sale for a Deputy Minister colt out of Isle Go West. This was a huge achievement for Gerry, Bridie, Quincy and all the team at that time selling for $1.8M. Gerry agreed to sponsor my visa for the summer of 2000 and I ended up staying with him for two years. While there I gained valuable experience with Gerry and the team prepping yearlings, foaling mares and getting mares in foal. This is where I truly learned and improved my horsemanship skills. It was difficult to leave, but my time at Dromoland catapulted me to greater things. After leaving Gerry I became Assistant Farm Manager at Ned Evans Spring Hill Farm in Virginia. Later I travelled the globe from Argentina to New Zealand and Japan, until I eventually settled back in Ireland, now managing Godolphin’s Ballysheehan and Victor Studs.” Willie Brogan (Ballysheehan & Victor Studs, Cashel, Co. Tipperary) Farm Manager “I cannot begin to put into words how much I owe Gerry, for the opportunities he gave me. He was an outstanding horseman and the most generous man you could meet for giving time to help young people. From the day I arrived at Dromoland, he never stopped believing in me and is the sole reason that I got accepted onto Godolphin Flying Start. Without his belief, I would most definitely not be where I am today. Since 2014, I have been fortunate enough to travel the world in the racing industry, and it didn’t matter where I was, somebody would fondly regale a story of Dromoland and Gerry Dilger. There are an endless list of people in the industry worldwide, thriving on the wisdom that Gerry imparted. He had a wonderful knack for spotting potential in both equine & human talent. A rare ability to put faith in young people and know that they would reward him for his time down the line. The best boss I have had to-date – his ability to stretch and challenge you, yet give comfort that he always had your back, resulted in countless people developing their horsemanship under his tutelage. Working at Dromoland was never just a job, it was embracing an extended family. Mentors like Gerry are rare, out in the lunge pit with the Ralph Lauren sleeves rolled up, telling us “you can’t buy those hands in Walmart”, but I couldn’t think of a more fitting way than this scholarship, to ensure Gerry’s legacy lives on.” Emma Pugsley (Dromoland 2014) Nominations, Coolmore Australia “Dromoland Farm was a home away from home. Gerry was not only an incredible horseman, but a tremendous teacher and leader to all who spent time on the farm. Gerry took great pride in educating and training the next generation. What we learned went far beyond horsemanship and breeding skills. He helped us all grow as individuals through encouragement and support. Gerry was a very humble man who put all his achievements down to luck. However, ask anyone who worked for him and they’ll say it was down to his hard working attitude, passion and an extraordinary eye for a horse. He was a leader who led by example. He was extremely hands-on on the farm and I feel incredibly lucky to have been able to learn from him.” Sylvia O'Donnell (Dromoland Farm 2017) Shipping, BBA Ireland "Squeezing the meaning of the year I spent working for Gerry at Dromoland into just a few sentences won't do it justice but I'll try anyway. That year was one of the best years of my life. From the beginning I received training and mentorship in so many facets of an operating stud farm on a level that can be rare in this industry. Gerry's immense generosity, his wonderful skill for pinhooking and an amazing experience that allowed me to prep an eventual Kentucky Derby winner are just some of the things that I will be eternally grateful for." Carol Kelly (Dromoland Farm 2014-2015) Stud Hand, Rheinwood Pastoral (Australia) “I spent a summer in Dromoland in 2015 and needless to say I loved every minute of it. When you hear anyone speak about Gerry, it’s always in legendary terms, simply because he was a legend. I’ve been back to Kentucky every November since 2015 and Gerry was always there for a word of advice and support when it was needed. As he used to say himself about a good horse, he was simply “Different gravy” Tom Fogarty Nominations Sales Executive, Kildangan Stud “Working at Dromoland Farm set me on a career path in the Equine Nutrition Industry. Gerry took the time to teach me many skills in my early days at Dromoland, but it was Gerry’s willingness to help someone starting out that left a lasting impression on me. Gerry encouraged me to pursue my interest in nutrition and was hugely supportive when I commenced work in this field.” Joanne Hurley M.Agr. SC, (Dromoland Farm 1999 – 2002) Irish Country Manager, GAIN Equine Nutrition, Ireland “Gerry Dilger was a brilliant horseman and absolute gentleman. I have nothing but admiration for Gerry in how he carved out a career in the industry. From completing the Irish National Stud Course and climbing the ladder to own and manage Dromoland Farm is incredible. The number of people that have gone on to long and successful careers in the thoroughbred industry having spent time at Dromoland Farm speaks volumes about the kind man Gerry was. Gerry’s work ethic and enthusiasm in the barn was infectious, he got the best out of his staff and would do anything for those that were willing to learn and to progress in their careers. His eye for a horse and attention to detail in caring for them was second to none, you don’t raise and sell back-to-back Kentucky Derby winners by chance. Gerry’s ability to pass on his knowledge and teach others has helped so many of us and the scholarship is a fitting tribute to his legacy, I know he would be very proud. I will always be grateful that I can call Gerry both a mentor and friend.” Nick Cope Manager at Stanley Lodge Stud Gallery Quick Links Home About Contact Us Resources Applications News Sitemap © 2026 The Gerry Dilger Equine Scholarship Foundation. Diamond History of the Connemara Pony News Contact Us Menu Toggle Contact Details How to Find Us Internships for 2026 Teddy and Harry, Class 1 Stallions.   Applications are now open for Student Internships at our yard here in Connemara for 2026. We have a yard that is a Stud Farm and Breeding Facility where we operate exclusively by Artificial Insemination, all aspects from Stallion collection, equine semen processing to mare preparation and Insemination are included. Handling mares and mares with foals and youngsters. Lots of opportunity to learn about all aspects of the Breeding side. E.U. Approved semen collection and storage centre. Youngsters are trained on site for sales and competition including Show jumping and Working Hunter. A.I.R.E. approved Equestrian Centre also on site, opportunity to learn and help with that side also.   Suitable applicant would carry out all normal yard duties, cleaning stables, feeding, exercising, etc. Suitable Applicants must be confident handling and being around horses, be 18 years +, have a good level of English, with no old injuries or allergies / ailments. Sociable and friendly, able to work off own initiative and as part of a Team. Experienced riders will also have the opportunity to ride and perhaps compete. Minimum stay is 4 weeks. Accommodation is on site.   Can be processed as part of and in association with University Internship experience. 78.text: Working Student | Equestrian Job Post a job Working Student Hotel Reiterhof Altmühlsee , Gunzenhausen, Bayern, Germany Job Employer Map Pay from 550 € month Work Type Temporary Work Time Full-time Live-In Live-In Required Bring Own Horse Training Available Non-Smoker Job No 428983 Status Looking Updated 28/05/2026 Expires in 15 days Views 2,350 Working Student assisting with riding, training, teaching, feeding and all other stable and farm chores This position will be available June through October 2026, with a minimum stay of 1 month required. Please let us know your time preference in advance. We are additionally looking for interns to support our hotel/restaurant/kitchen staff located on our equestrian facility. This gives those interested, the opportunity to work a combination program of working 1/2 day with the horses and 1/2 day in the hospitality sector. So there are lots of options for adventurous people or to bring a non-riding friend/partner along to work in our team.
